Start with: \"of the things on your plate, which one feels stuck?\" Force prioritisation. Don't assume he's tracking previous action items; check explicitly.\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/skills/1-on-1-prep/SKILL.md:54:- Frustration they haven't voiced in Slack/Monday\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/reports/marketing-repair-profitability-brief.md:4:**Source:** Team agent analysis of Monday.com + Shopify + GSC data\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:1:# Michael Ferrari — Performance Audit\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:6:**Data Sources:** Monday.com (Board 18393875720), Intercom (admin@icorrect.co.uk / Support account)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:7:**Monday Board:** \"Ferrari's Daily Work\" — 462 items total, daily groups with time tracking\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:13:Ferrari logs 6.8–8.6 hours/day on his Monday board. The single largest bucket is \"Clients\" at 3–4.2 hours daily. **Intercom data does not support this volume.** CS Jarvis's direct API audit found ~31 manual customer-facing emails and ~12 internal notes across the week — estimated at **~75 minutes of actual composition time.** Many messages attributed to the \"Support\" account are n8n automation (device received, repair complete, courier booked), not Ferrari's manual work. Meanwhile, \"Clients\" accounts for ~18 hours across the week.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:17:Revenue-generating work (quoting, booking, invoicing) accounts for just **9% of Ferrari's 765 Monday board actions**. The remaining 91% is board admin, BM logistics, workshop intake, and data entry. He's wearing 24 different hats and the one that makes money gets the least attention. The gap between logged time and verifiable output is the core finding of this audit.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:21:## 2. MONDAY.COM — TIME TRACKING ANALYSIS\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:23:### Weekly Summary (Board: Ferrari's Daily Work)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:68:| 9702337 | Support | Admin | Shared admin account — Ferrari sends from this |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:70:Ferrari sends emails as \"Support\" (admin@icorrect.co.uk), not from a personal admin account. This means there is no way to distinguish his activity from any other admin using the same account.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:84:Two possible untracked conversions — Jordan Clark (\"I have booked online #U2TJF8MTC\") and Nizam Kabir (\"Thanks, I've now booked\") — both say they booked but neither got a `create-repair` tag. The Shopify webhook isn't reliably tagging all conversions, meaning some bookings may be slipping through without Monday items being created.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:88:**CRITICAL DISTINCTION:** Many messages sent from the \"Support\" account are **n8n automation**, not Ferrari typing. Only messages without `app_package_code: \"n8n-automations-nkor\"` are manual.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:90:| What n8n automates | What Ferrari types manually |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:103:| Internal notes (Monday item links, etc.) | ~12 | ~12 min |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:161:Warranty appointment confirmation for 26 Feb 9:30am. Tagged `create-repair` → n8n created Monday item. The one conversation this week showing proper end-to-end workflow (call → email → tag → Monday item).\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:163:**Note:** The emails Ferrari does write are well-crafted — detailed diagnostic findings, proper pricing, YouTube links, courier options. Quality is not the issue. Volume and speed are.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:213:| Metric | Logged (Monday) | Verified (Intercom) |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:228:- **Ferrari on annual leave** at the end of the previous week (pre-Feb 18). Enquiries from Feb 18-19 had neither Fin nor Ferrari responding.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:229:- **Feb 23-25 Ferrari was back and logging 7-8h/day** on Monday — but 18 contact forms still went unanswered.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:230:- **The lead bleed predates the Fin disable.** This is a Ferrari responsiveness issue, not a Fin outage issue.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:234:1. **Phone calls not captured.** Ferrari handles inbound calls. These don't show in Intercom unless he creates a follow-up email. If so, only 1 call (Vimal) resulted in a documented follow-up this week.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:236:2. **Time tracker left running.** The Monday.com time tracking may be started and not stopped, inflating hours.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:240:4. **Batch working pattern.** CS Jarvis audit shows Ferrari works in bursts — Mon 23rd had 14 messages, Tue/Wed 11-13 each, but Thu-Fri near zero. Leads from early in the week sit for days.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:246:## 5. MONDAY.COM MAIN BOARD ACTIVITY (Board 349212843)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:248:**Ferrari user ID:** 55780786\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:255:| Automation (Monday internal, user -4) | 2,678 |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:257:| **Ferrari** | **765** |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:264:### Ferrari Daily Breakdown\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:276:**Pattern matches Intercom:** Friday near-zero, Monday catch-up burst.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:278:### n8n Automation Running Under Ferrari's Profile\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:280:**16 of Ferrari's 46 item creations are n8n automation**, not manual work. The Intercom `create-repair` → Monday workflow uses Ferrari's API token, so items it creates are logged under his user ID.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:303:Each n8n creation also triggers ~26 rapid-fire column updates (Device, Client, Repair Type, etc.) within seconds — all logged under Ferrari. **~42 of Ferrari's 765 actions (5%) are pure automation.**\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:307:### Ferrari's Actual Manual Work (main board)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:309:After removing n8n automation, Ferrari's manual board work breaks down as:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:330:### Status Transitions Ferrari Makes\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:346:**Key insight:** The single biggest status action is \"Return Booked\" (39) — Back Market returns. Combined with BM-related actions (210 total across all event types), **Back Market logistics accounts for ~27% of Ferrari's main board activity.** This is admin/logistics work, not client conversion work.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:350:Ferrari's main board activity is **legitimate and substantial** — 150 actions/day, creating items, routing repairs, managing couriers, assigning technicians. This is real work. However:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:359:## 6. FERRARI MOVEMENT PATTERNS — WHAT PULLS HIM INTO CHAOS\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:363:Analysis of minute-by-minute Monday main board actions mapped by item type (Client, BM, n8n, Corporate, Parts):\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:390:Monday 23 Feb is the worst: **132 context switches across 53 different items** — switching tasks every 3-4 minutes. Wed 25 Feb had the most focused bursts (19) but still 68 switches.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:394:### The 11:41 Chaos Window (Monday 23 Feb)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:429:**77 unique BM items touched across the audit week.** Each goes through a multi-step lifecycle (receive → diagnose → repair → track → ship) and Ferrari is managing all of it.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:431:### BM Real Time vs Spread Time (Monday 23 Feb)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:442:**Total actual BM work: ~15 minutes.** Buying labels, pasting tracking numbers into Monday, clicking batch status changes. That's the entire BM workload for the day.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:444:But it's **smeared across 08:39 to 18:04 — the entire 9.5-hour working day.** Between every BM micro-task, Ferrari bounces to client items, n8n items, corporate items, then back to BM. The result is 132 context switches and zero sustained focus on anything.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:457:The CS Jarvis finding of **45h average response time** maps directly to this pattern: morning enquiries don't get touched until afternoon, and Thu/Fri arrivals sit until Monday.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:461:Ferrari isn't idle — he's **doing the wrong work at the wrong time.** The morning hours (when enquiries arrive and response speed matters most for conversion) are consumed by BM return logistics and courier dispatch. By the time he gets to Intercom, the leads are cold.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:470:## 7. FERRARI'S COMPLETE TASK BREAKDOWN\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:472:Ferrari touched **130 unique items** across the audit week, performing **765 actions**. Below is every type of work he does, broken down by item type, depth of engagement, and job function.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:476:| Item Type | Items | Actions | What Ferrari Does |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:481:| n8n (Intercom→Monday) | 19 | 140 | Completing the item setup that n8n starts |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:518:19 items arrived from Intercom via the n8n `create-repair` workflow. Each arrives with just a name and order number. Ferrari then manually fills in: Device, Requested Repairs, Repair Type, Client type, Custom Products, Status, and sometimes Tracking, Passcode, and Info Capture.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:531:Most BM items are 2–5 clicks. The batch at 11:43 on Monday — 10 items going to \"Return Booked\" simultaneously — took literally 1 second.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:533:### Monday Updates & Team Communication\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:535:Ferrari posted **37 updates** on the main board in the audit week:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:537:| Update Type | Count | Written by Ferrari? |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:546:**32 of 37 updates are automation. Ferrari manually wrote 5 updates.**\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:569:**The revenue-generating work — quoting, booking, invoicing — is 9% of Ferrari's week.** 67 actions out of 765.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:571:### Ferrari's Daily Task List (Every Hat He Wears)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:574:2. **Paste tracking numbers** into Monday from Royal Mail\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:576:4. **Complete n8n items** — fill in 8-12 fields on each Intercom→Monday item\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:593:21. **BM customer comms & counteroffers** — negotiating with Back Market customers on pricing/returns *(added by Ferrari)*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:594:22. **IC checks** — iCloud status checks on incoming BM devices *(added by Ferrari)*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:595:23. **SKU checks** — verifying Back Market SKUs/product listings *(added by Ferrari)*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:596:24. **Emails** — reading, triaging, and responding to Intercom inbox *(added by Ferrari)*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:598:*Tasks 21–24 added by Ferrari on review. Note: task 24 (\"Emails\") is the activity audited in Section 3 — ~31 manual emails / ~75 min composition time in the audit week (CS Jarvis verified). The gap between Ferrari's perception of email workload and the verified output is a key finding of this audit.*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:606:**Ferrari extension:** 205\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:615:| Ferrari (Ext 205) answered | 1,134 (61% of all answered) |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:616:| Average calls/week (Ferrari) | 45 |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:620:### Ferrari Call Volume — Weekly Trend\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:679:- Ferrari was responsible for the rollout but was not maintaining or improving it\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:693:3. **Require Intercom conversation links.** Every client task on Monday must link to the corresponding Intercom conversation or have a note explaining what happened.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:697:4. **Implement conversion tracking.** Track: Enquiry → Quote Sent → Booking Made → Device Received → Repair Complete → Payment. This is the pipeline Ferrari should be managing.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:701:6. **Separate admin accounts.** Ferrari should have his own Intercom admin account, not share \"Support\". This makes individual activity trackable.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:705:7. **Define measurable KPIs for Ferrari's role:**\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:712:8. **Regular audit cadence.** Weekly cross-reference of Monday time tracking vs Intercom output, automated via agent.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:714:9. **Migrate n8n/agent automations to a service account.** Currently n8n workflows use individual API tokens (Ferrari's for Intercom→Monday, Ricky's for BM→Monday). All automation should run under a dedicated service account (e.g., \"Systems Manager\" or new \"Automation\" user) so human activity audits aren't polluted by bot actions.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:716:10. **Separate Ferrari's personal board categories.** The current \"Clients\" bucket should be broken into: Client Calls, Client Emails, Quotes, BM Admin, Courier Management, Board Admin. This matches what the main board activity data shows he's actually doing.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:724:| Source | What It Shows | Ferrari's Verified Output |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:726:| **Monday personal board** (self-reported) | 18h/week on \"Clients\" | Unverifiable — single opaque bucket |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:729:| **Monday main board** (activity logs) | 765 actions, 150 items touched | Legitimate admin work — but not \"Clients\" |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:730:| **Monday movement patterns** (minute-by-minute) | 132 context switches on peak day, BM dominates mornings | Enquiries ignored 08:00–14:00 while doing BM logistics |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:738:| Monday board admin (status changes, etc.) | 2-3h |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:740:| **Monday logged total** | **~38h/week** |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:748:- Shared \"Support\" Intercom account (admin 9702337) — some messages attributed to Ferrari may be from other admins. /home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/main/workspace/state/sessions/agent%3Acodex%3Aacp%3Aa4a14a7b-c134-49c3-986f-fb2d898890d2%3Aoneshot%3A750490d1-07ae-44ed-a6b5-f56a5a1bd579.json:1156:              "stdout": "# Monday.com Board Reference — iCorrect\n\n**Last updated:** 16 Feb 2026\n**Purpose:** Permanent reference for pulling team performance reports. READ THIS before querying the board.\n\n---\n\n## API Access\n\n```bash\nsource /home/ricky/config/api-keys/.env\n# Token: $MONDAY_API_TOKEN\n# Endpoint: https://api.monday.com/v2 (GraphQL POST)\n```\n\n---\n\n## Boards\n\n| Board | ID | Purpose |\n|-------|----|---------|\n| **iCorrect Main** | `349212843` | All repairs, trade-ins, client orders — the central board |\n| **BM Devices 2024** | `3892194968` | Back Market trade-in tracking (linked to main board items) |\n| **Ferrari Timetable V2** | `9970464581` | Ferrari's self-reported work hours |\n| **Ferrari Daily Tasks** | `18393875720` | Ferrari's task-level time tracking |\n\n---\n\n## User IDs\n\n| Person | User ID | Role |\n|--------|---------|------|\n| Andres Egas | `49001724` | Refurb Tech |\n| Safan Patel | `25304513` | Lead Repair Tech |\n| Michael Ferrari | `55780786` | Client Services (Remote) |\n| Adil Azad | `94961618` | Customer Service / Intake |\n| Mykhailo (Misha) | `64642914` | Lead Refurb Tech |\n| Roni Mykhailiuk | `79665360` | QC & Parts |\n| Ricky Panesar | `1034414` | Owner |\n| Systems Manager | `12304876` | Automation account |\n| Client Services | `32191305` | Shared account |\n| **Automations** | `-4` | Monday.com system (automations, integrations) |\n\n**⚠️ User ID `-4` = automations.** Filter this out when counting human activity.\n\n---\n\n## Main Board Groups (349212843)\n\n### Active Queues (for performance tracking)\n\n| Group | ID | Who Works It |\n|-------|----|-------------|\n| **Safan (Short Deadline)** | `new_group81510__1` | Safan — **primary repair queue, count this one** |\n| Safan (Long Deadline) | `new_group84838__1` | Safan — lower priority, **ignore for weekly reports** |\n| Andres | `new_group99626` | Andres |\n| Mykhailo | `new_group97788__1` | Misha |\n| Roni | `group_mktmskq5` | Roni |\n| Adil | `group_mkx5bd07` | Adil |\n| Ferrari | `new_group34603` | Ferrari |\n\n### Pipeline Groups\n\n| Group | ID | Purpose |\n|-------|----|---------|\n| New Orders | `new_group77101__1` | Incoming confirmed orders |\n| New Orders (Not Confirmed) | `new_group32262__1` | Unconfirmed bookings |\n| Today's Repairs | `new_group70029` | Daily work queue |\n| Incoming Future | `new_group34198` | Scheduled future repairs |\n| Awaiting Confirmation of Price | `new_group72011` | Quote sent, waiting for client |\n| Trade-In BMs Awaiting Validation | `group_mktsw34v` | BM trade-ins needing diagnostic |\n\n### Process Groups\n\n| Group | ID | Purpose |\n|-------|----|---------|\n| Quality Control | `group_mkwkapa6` | Roni's QC queue |\n| BMs Awaiting Sale | `new_group88387__1` | Diagnosed, repaired, ready to list |\n| Awaiting Parts | `group_mkrcpnpb` | Blocked on parts |\n| Client Services - To Do | `new_group6580` | Ferrari's task queue |\n| Client Services - Awaiting Confirmation | `new_group56509` | Waiting on client response |\n| Outbound Shipping | `new_group77650__1` | Ready to ship |\n| Awaiting Collection | `new_group34086` | Client to collect |\n\n### End-State Groups\n\n| Group | ID | Purpose |\n|-------|----|---------|\n| Completed Refurbs | `new_group32315` | Done refurbs |\n| Returned | `new_group25337` | Returned to client |\n| Cancelled/Missed Bookings | `new_group30399__1` | Cancelled |\n| Devices In Hole | `group_mkt5bdbb` | Long-term stuck (97 items — legacy) |\n| Devices Left in Long Term | `long_term_contact` | Abandoned devices |\n| Recycle List | `new_group69818` | To recycle |\n| Locked | `group_mkr52kwr` | iCloud locked |\n\n---\n\n## Key Columns (Main Board)\n\n### Status Columns\n\n**`status4` — \"Status\"** (the main status column)\n```\n0: Awaiting Confirmation    105: Awaiting Part\n1: Received                 106: Booking Confirmed\n2: Diagnostic Complete      107: QC Failure\n3: Courier Booked           108: Book Courier\n5: New Repair               109: Password Req\n6: Returned                 110: Ready to Quote\n7: Clamped                  151: Under Refurb\n8: Quote Sent               152: Cleaning\n9: BER/Parts                153: Client Contacted\n10: Client To Contact       154: Cancelled/Declined\n11: Invoiced                155: Battery Testing\n12: Under Repair            156: To Ship\n13: Diagnostics             157: Software Install\n14: Repair Paused           158: Purchased\n15: Reassemble              159: Missing Parts\n16: Repaired                160: Shipped\n17: Part Repaired\n18: Expecting Device\n19: Return Booked\n101: Error\n102: Ready To Collect\n103: Book Return Courier\n104: Queued For Repair\n```\n\n**BM Diagnostic Flow (status4):**\n- Start: `1: Received` → `13: Diagnostics` or `152: Cleaning`\n- End: `2: Diagnostic Complete` or `104: Queued For Repair`\n- To measure diagnostic time: track when status4 changes FROM Received TO Diagnostics/Cleaning (start), and when it changes TO Diagnostic Complete/Queued For Repair (end)\n- **Use activity logs to see WHO made the status change — that's the real \"diagnosed by\"**\n\n**`service` — \"Service\"** (how device arrived)\n```\n0: Gophr Courier    5: Unconfirmed       14: Mail-In\n1: Walk-In          6: External Mail-In\n2: Stuart Courier   7: International Mail-In\n4: Gophr Express    8: Internal\n```\n\n**`color_mkypbg6z` — \"Trade-in Status\"**\n```\n0: Sold       4: Purchased    8: N/a\n1: To-List    6: Counter      9: Manual Review\n2: Trade-in   7: Listed       11: Error\n3: Pay-Out\n```\n\n**`status_177` — \"Tech Repair Phase\"**\n```\n0: Repair                    3: Diagnostics\n1: Pre-Testing/Disassembly   4: Repaired\n2: Post-Testing/Assembly     5: Not Started\n                             6: Quotation\n```\n\n**`status_110` — \"Tech Repair Status\"**\n```\n0: Stuck with Repair    3: Jump to Other Repair\n1: Active               4: Battery Testing\n2: Complete             5: Not Started\n                        11: No/Incorrect Password\n                        15: Parts Issue\n```\n\n### Time Tracking Columns\n\n| Column ID | Title | What It Tracks |\n|-----------|-------|---------------|\n| `time_tracking` | Diagnostic Time | Time spent on diagnostic |\n| `duration_mkyrykvn` | Cleaning Time | Time spent cleaning |\n| `time_tracking9` | Repair Time | Time spent on repair |\n| `time_tracking93` | Refurb Time | Time spent on refurb |\n| `time_tracking98` | Total Time | Total time on device |\n\n**Format:** `{\"running\": \"false\", \"duration\": 3795, \"startDate\": 1768909537}` — duration is in **seconds**.\n\n### People Columns\n\n| Column ID | Title | Notes |\n|-----------|-------|-------|\n| `person` | Technician | Assigned repair tech |\n| `multiple_person_mkyp6fdz` | Diagnosed By | **⚠️ NOT RELIABLE — often empty** |\n| `multiple_person_mkwqj321` | Diagnostic (people) | Who did diagnostic |\n| `multiple_person_mkwqsxse` | Refurb (people) | Who did refurb |\n| `multiple_person_mkwqy930` | Repair (people) | Who did repair |\n| `multiple_person_mkyp2bka` | QC By | Who did QC |\n\n**⚠️ \"Diagnosed By\" (`multiple_person_mkyp6fdz`) is unreliable.** Don't use it as primary attribution. Instead, use activity logs to see who changed the status column on that item.\n\n### Date Columns\n\n| Column ID | Title | Use |\n|-----------|-------|-----|\n| `date4` | Received | When device was received |\n| `date36` | **Deadline** | Repair deadline |\n| `collection_date` | Date Repaired | When repair completed |\n| `date_mkwdmm9k` | Diag. Complete | When diagnostic finished |\n| `date_mkwdwx03` | Quote Sent | When quote was sent |\n| `date_mkwdan7z` | Repaired | When marked repaired |\n| `date_mkypmgfc` | Intake Timestamp | When intake happened |\n| `date_mkypt8db` | QC Time | When QC done |\n| `date_mkq385pa` | Date Listed (BM) | When listed on Back Market |\n| `date_mkq34t04` | Date Sold (BM) | When sold on Back Market |\n| `date_mkq3gbbtp` | Date Purchased (BM) | When purchased as trade-in |\n| `creation_log4` | Created | Item creation date |\n| `last_updated__1` | Updated | Last update timestamp |\n\n---\n\n## BM Board Groups (3892194968)\n\n| Group | ID | Purpose |\n|-------|----|---------|\n| BM Trade-Ins | `group_mkq3wkeq` | Incoming trade-ins |\n| BM To List / Listed / Sold | `new_group` | Pipeline to sale |\n| Devices to Refurbish | `new_group62345__1` | Needs refurb work |\n| BM Returns | `new_group_mkmybfgr` | Customer returns |\n| Rejected / iC Locked | `new_group_Mjj6tJYg` | Can't process |\n| Shipped | `new_group269` | Completed sales |\n\n---\n\n## How to Pull Reports\n\n### 1. Weekly Team Performance Report\n\n**What to measure per person:**\n- First event timestamp → start time\n- Last event timestamp → end time  \n- Total `update_column_value` events → activity volume\n- Status changes to `Repaired` (index 16) or `Diagnostic Complete` (index 2) → completions\n- Lunch gap → largest gap between events in 11:00-14:00 window\n\n**Query pattern (one day, one user):**\n```graphql\n{\n  boards(ids: 349212843) {\n    activity_logs(\n      from: \"2026-02-10T00:00:00Z\"\n      to: \"2026-02-10T23:59:59Z\"\n      limit: 500\n      user_ids: [25304513]\n    ) {\n      event entity data user_id created_at\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**⚠️ API limit: 500 events per call.** For high-activity users (Adil, Andres) or busy days, query day-by-day not week-at-once.\n\n**`created_at` format:** First 13 digits = milliseconds epoch (UTC). Example: `17707466307689316` → use first 13: `1770746630768` → `new Date(1770746630768)`.\n\n**UTC = UK local time** (no daylight saving in winter months, Oct-Mar).\n\n### 2. Completions Count\n",
```

/home/ricky/.openclaw/shared/PROBLEMS.md:117:No proper inventory system. Parts can run out without warning, which directly blocks repairs and costs the business money. Inventory forecasting nearly caused serious damage to the business this year. Roni manages parts but doesn't have good visibility or a simple enough system in Monday.com for intake — especially for China shipments from Nancy.
/home/ricky/.openclaw/shared/PROBLEMS.md:119:**What we need:** A parts tracking system with visibility into current stock, forecasted demand, and automated alerts before things run out. A simple intake process for both individual parts and bulk China shipments. Integration with repair queue data so parts availability feeds into job scheduling.

Attribution works as follows:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:28:Signature matching (\"Kind regards, Michael\" / \"— Michael Ferrari\") was evaluated but deemed unnecessary: the n8n exclusion filter is sufficient to isolate human replies per the Feb 2026 audit. Cross-referencing with Monday active hours adds noise without improving accuracy and was not implemented.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:30:**Known limitation:** If another human agent ever uses the shared Support account, their replies will be misattributed to Ferrari. This has not been observed historically.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:37:| `intercom_avg_response_time_hrs` | Mean time (hours) from customer's first message in each conversation to Ferrari's first human reply. Only conversations with a measurable first-reply time are included. |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:73:| `leads_replied_personally` | Count where Ferrari (Support account, human, non-automated) sent at least one reply |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:74:| `leads_converted_to_quote` | N/A — requires Intercom-to-Monday join key (see below) |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:75:| `leads_converted_to_job` | N/A — requires Intercom-to-Monday join key (see below) |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:77:**Known limitation (conversion tracking):** There is no reliable join key linking an Intercom conversation to a Monday job. The Feb 2026 audit found Ferrari achieves ~100% conversion when handling leads personally (4/4) vs ~9% when Fin AI handles alone. To wire this up, either:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:78:- Add a Monday item ID to Intercom conversation notes at the point of booking, or\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:89:| `cs_utilisation_pct` | `gross_hours / CS_WORKING_HOURS * 100` where `CS_WORKING_HOURS = 8` | Gross hours from Monday activity log (first event to last event on the board). Values >100% indicate days longer than 8h. Does not account for actual CS task time (Intercom + calls) because per-message time data is unavailable. |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:90:| `revenue_attributed` | N/A | Would require a traceable ID linking a lead/Intercom conversation to a paid job on the Monday board. Not currently available. |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:98:| `start_time` | First Monday board activity — valid proxy for start of shift |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:99:| `finish_time` | Last Monday board activity — valid proxy for end of shift |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:101:| `items_touched` | Monday board interactions; reframed as \"Monday board items touched\" rather than repairs |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:102:| `meeting_mins` | Meeting time parsed from Ferrari's board |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:119:**Note on response times:** Average response times of 52-88 hours reflect conversations opened days or weeks earlier that Ferrari replies to on the given day — not same-day response latency. Median is a better day-level indicator.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:126:# Ferrari (CS mode — produces _with_cs_metrics.csv)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:127:python3 scripts/team_daily_csv.py --person ferrari --from 2026-04-07 --to 2026-04-13\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/skills/performance-review/SKILL.md:17:2. **Monday board 349212843**: aggregate activity data\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/skills/performance-review/SKILL.md:43:- Any friction signals? (Slack tone, Monday comment patterns, queue conflicts)\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/AGENTS.md:35:- **Scope**: Run `scripts/monday_tech_daily_stats.py` for yesterday's data. Generate daily pulse.\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/AGENTS.md:64:  - Monday status simplification (single \"In Progress\" state, not implemented)\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/AGENTS.md:109:- `scripts/`: Monday API scripts (daily stats, analysis tools)\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/skills/1-on-1-prep/SKILL.md:49:**Ferrari**: he loses track of threads. Start with: \"of the things on your plate, which one feels stuck?\" Force prioritisation. Don't assume he's tracking previous action items; check explicitly.\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/skills/1-on-1-prep/SKILL.md:54:- Frustration they haven't voiced in Slack/Monday\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/reports/marketing-repair-profitability-brief.md:4:**Source:** Team agent analysis of Monday.com + Shopify + GSC data\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:1:# Michael Ferrari — Performance Audit\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:6:**Data Sources:** Monday.com (Board 18393875720), Intercom (admin@icorrect.co.uk / Support account)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:7:**Monday Board:** \"Ferrari's Daily Work\" — 462 items total, daily groups with time tracking\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:13:Ferrari logs 6.8–8.6 hours/day on his Monday board. The single largest bucket is \"Clients\" at 3–4.2 hours daily. **Intercom data does not support this volume.** CS Jarvis's direct API audit found ~31 manual customer-facing emails and ~12 internal notes across the week — estimated at **~75 minutes of actual composition time.** Many messages attributed to the \"Support\" account are n8n automation (device received, repair complete, courier booked), not Ferrari's manual work. Meanwhile, \"Clients\" accounts for ~18 hours across the week.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:17:Revenue-generating work (quoting, booking, invoicing) accounts for just **9% of Ferrari's 765 Monday board actions**. The remaining 91% is board admin, BM logistics, workshop intake, and data entry. He's wearing 24 different hats and the one that makes money gets the least attention. The gap between logged time and verifiable output is the core finding of this audit.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:21:## 2. MONDAY.COM — TIME TRACKING ANALYSIS\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:23:### Weekly Summary (Board: Ferrari's Daily Work)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:68:| 9702337 | Support | Admin | Shared admin account — Ferrari sends from this |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:70:Ferrari sends emails as \"Support\" (admin@icorrect.co.uk), not from a personal admin account. This means there is no way to distinguish his activity from any other admin using the same account.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:84:Two possible untracked conversions — Jordan Clark (\"I have booked online #U2TJF8MTC\") and Nizam Kabir (\"Thanks, I've now booked\") — both say they booked but neither got a `create-repair` tag. The Shopify webhook isn't reliably tagging all conversions, meaning some bookings may be slipping through without Monday items being created.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:88:**CRITICAL DISTINCTION:** Many messages sent from the \"Support\" account are **n8n automation**, not Ferrari typing. Only messages without `app_package_code: \"n8n-automations-nkor\"` are manual.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:90:| What n8n automates | What Ferrari types manually |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:103:| Internal notes (Monday item links, etc.) | ~12 | ~12 min |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:161:Warranty appointment confirmation for 26 Feb 9:30am. Tagged `create-repair` → n8n created Monday item. The one conversation this week showing proper end-to-end workflow (call → email → tag → Monday item).\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:163:**Note:** The emails Ferrari does write are well-crafted — detailed diagnostic findings, proper pricing, YouTube links, courier options. Quality is not the issue. Volume and speed are.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:213:| Metric | Logged (Monday) | Verified (Intercom) |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:228:- **Ferrari on annual leave** at the end of the previous week (pre-Feb 18). Enquiries from Feb 18-19 had neither Fin nor Ferrari responding.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:229:- **Feb 23-25 Ferrari was back and logging 7-8h/day** on Monday — but 18 contact forms still went unanswered.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:230:- **The lead bleed predates the Fin disable.** This is a Ferrari responsiveness issue, not a Fin outage issue.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:234:1. **Phone calls not captured.** Ferrari handles inbound calls. These don't show in Intercom unless he creates a follow-up email. If so, only 1 call (Vimal) resulted in a documented follow-up this week.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:236:2. **Time tracker left running.** The Monday.com time tracking may be started and not stopped, inflating hours.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:240:4. **Batch working pattern.** CS Jarvis audit shows Ferrari works in bursts — Mon 23rd had 14 messages, Tue/Wed 11-13 each, but Thu-Fri near zero. Leads from early in the week sit for days.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:246:## 5. MONDAY.COM MAIN BOARD ACTIVITY (Board 349212843)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:248:**Ferrari user ID:** 55780786\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:255:| Automation (Monday internal, user -4) | 2,678 |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:257:| **Ferrari** | **765** |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:264:### Ferrari Daily Breakdown\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:276:**Pattern matches Intercom:** Friday near-zero, Monday catch-up burst.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:278:### n8n Automation Running Under Ferrari's Profile\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:280:**16 of Ferrari's 46 item creations are n8n automation**, not manual work. The Intercom `create-repair` → Monday workflow uses Ferrari's API token, so items it creates are logged under his user ID.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:303:Each n8n creation also triggers ~26 rapid-fire column updates (Device, Client, Repair Type, etc.) within seconds — all logged under Ferrari. **~42 of Ferrari's 765 actions (5%) are pure automation.**\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:307:### Ferrari's Actual Manual Work (main board)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:309:After removing n8n automation, Ferrari's manual board work breaks down as:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:330:### Status Transitions Ferrari Makes\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:346:**Key insight:** The single biggest status action is \"Return Booked\" (39) — Back Market returns. Combined with BM-related actions (210 total across all event types), **Back Market logistics accounts for ~27% of Ferrari's main board activity.** This is admin/logistics work, not client conversion work.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:350:Ferrari's main board activity is **legitimate and substantial** — 150 actions/day, creating items, routing repairs, managing couriers, assigning technicians. This is real work. However:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:359:## 6. FERRARI MOVEMENT PATTERNS — WHAT PULLS HIM INTO CHAOS\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:363:Analysis of minute-by-minute Monday main board actions mapped by item type (Client, BM, n8n, Corporate, Parts):\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:390:Monday 23 Feb is the worst: **132 context switches across 53 different items** — switching tasks every 3-4 minutes. Wed 25 Feb had the most focused bursts (19) but still 68 switches.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:394:### The 11:41 Chaos Window (Monday 23 Feb)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:429:**77 unique BM items touched across the audit week.** Each goes through a multi-step lifecycle (receive → diagnose → repair → track → ship) and Ferrari is managing all of it.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:431:### BM Real Time vs Spread Time (Monday 23 Feb)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:442:**Total actual BM work: ~15 minutes.** Buying labels, pasting tracking numbers into Monday, clicking batch status changes. That's the entire BM workload for the day.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:444:But it's **smeared across 08:39 to 18:04 — the entire 9.5-hour working day.** Between every BM micro-task, Ferrari bounces to client items, n8n items, corporate items, then back to BM. The result is 132 context switches and zero sustained focus on anything.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:457:The CS Jarvis finding of **45h average response time** maps directly to this pattern: morning enquiries don't get touched until afternoon, and Thu/Fri arrivals sit until Monday.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:461:Ferrari isn't idle — he's **doing the wrong work at the wrong time.** The morning hours (when enquiries arrive and response speed matters most for conversion) are consumed by BM return logistics and courier dispatch. By the time he gets to Intercom, the leads are cold.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:470:## 7. FERRARI'S COMPLETE TASK BREAKDOWN\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:472:Ferrari touched **130 unique items** across the audit week, performing **765 actions**. Below is every type of work he does, broken down by item type, depth of engagement, and job function.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:476:| Item Type | Items | Actions | What Ferrari Does |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:481:| n8n (Intercom→Monday) | 19 | 140 | Completing the item setup that n8n starts |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:518:19 items arrived from Intercom via the n8n `create-repair` workflow. Each arrives with just a name and order number. Ferrari then manually fills in: Device, Requested Repairs, Repair Type, Client type, Custom Products, Status, and sometimes Tracking, Passcode, and Info Capture.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:531:Most BM items are 2–5 clicks. The batch at 11:43 on Monday — 10 items going to \"Return Booked\" simultaneously — took literally 1 second.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:533:### Monday Updates & Team Communication\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:535:Ferrari posted **37 updates** on the main board in the audit week:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:537:| Update Type | Count | Written by Ferrari? |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:546:**32 of 37 updates are automation. Ferrari manually wrote 5 updates.**\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:569:**The revenue-generating work — quoting, booking, invoicing — is 9% of Ferrari's week.** 67 actions out of 765.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:571:### Ferrari's Daily Task List (Every Hat He Wears)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:574:2. **Paste tracking numbers** into Monday from Royal Mail\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:576:4. **Complete n8n items** — fill in 8-12 fields on each Intercom→Monday item\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:593:21. **BM customer comms & counteroffers** — negotiating with Back Market customers on pricing/returns *(added by Ferrari)*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:594:22. **IC checks** — iCloud status checks on incoming BM devices *(added by Ferrari)*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:595:23. **SKU checks** — verifying Back Market SKUs/product listings *(added by Ferrari)*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:596:24. **Emails** — reading, triaging, and responding to Intercom inbox *(added by Ferrari)*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:598:*Tasks 21–24 added by Ferrari on review. Note: task 24 (\"Emails\") is the activity audited in Section 3 — ~31 manual emails / ~75 min composition time in the audit week (CS Jarvis verified). The gap between Ferrari's perception of email workload and the verified output is a key finding of this audit.*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:606:**Ferrari extension:** 205\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:615:| Ferrari (Ext 205) answered | 1,134 (61% of all answered) |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:616:| Average calls/week (Ferrari) | 45 |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:620:### Ferrari Call Volume — Weekly Trend\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:679:- Ferrari was responsible for the rollout but was not maintaining or improving it\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:693:3. **Require Intercom conversation links.** Every client task on Monday must link to the corresponding Intercom conversation or have a note explaining what happened.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:697:4. **Implement conversion tracking.** Track: Enquiry → Quote Sent → Booking Made → Device Received → Repair Complete → Payment. This is the pipeline Ferrari should be managing.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:701:6. **Separate admin accounts.** Ferrari should have his own Intercom admin account, not share \"Support\". This makes individual activity trackable.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:705:7. **Define measurable KPIs for Ferrari's role:**\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:712:8. **Regular audit cadence.** Weekly cross-reference of Monday time tracking vs Intercom output, automated via agent.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:714:9. **Migrate n8n/agent automations to a service account.** Currently n8n workflows use individual API tokens (Ferrari's for Intercom→Monday, Ricky's for BM→Monday). Each tag adds a task to someone already burned out.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/adil/adil_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:231:| Ferrari coordination | ~15 min | ~3 messages/day, 5 min each |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/adil/adil_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:256:| Post Feb 18 (5 days) | 57 | 11.4 | Client intake, logistics, Ferrari coordination |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/adil/adil_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:298:| Ferrari coordination | ~30 | Invoices, refunds, client chasing, approvals |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/adil/adil_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:306:### Ferrari Dependency\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/adil/adil_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:308:~30 of Adil's 183 replies tag @Ferrari. Common patterns:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/adil/adil_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:318:Many of these are legitimate operational routing — Ferrari handles invoicing, refunds, and client email. But the volume of back-and-forth adds load to someone already burned out. Every \"@Ferrari\" tag is a task on Ferrari's plate. Some of these could be resolved without Ferrari if Adil had invoice access or client email capability.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/adil/adil_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:358:- **He routes heavily to Ferrari** — ~30 tags creating tasks for someone already burned out\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/adil/adil_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:385:| ~30 Ferrari tags | **Structural** | Heavy dependency, but partly how the role is designed |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/adil/adil_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:434:\"These are the non-negotiables: in the building by 09:00 every day, Saturday rota shared with the team, no unexplained short days. I'll measure it over the next 4 weeks. The Monday data tells me everything.\"\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/adil/adil_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:442:| Ferrari dependency | Give front desk invoice access / Keep routing | Reduce Ferrari load by giving front desk basic Xero/invoice access |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/adil/adil_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:444:| Transition period | Immediate / Overlap training | 2-week overlap minimum — new hire needs to learn Monday board, shipping process, BM receiving |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/adil/adil_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:454:| Ferrari tags | Reduce to <15/week | Update reply analysis |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/adil/adil_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:461:- Typeform data pulled via API for all 4 forms. query)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/mykhailo/mykhailo_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:13:Mykhailo's per-day output (3.4 items/day) is 21% below his baseline (4.3/day). Both missed Saturdays were legitimate (annual leave 14 Feb, sick 21 Feb). Daily team meetings (averaging 1h 15m) explain most of his \"late\" first Monday.com actions, and account for some of the output gap — the baseline had no daily meetings.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/mykhailo/mykhailo_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:18:2. **Monday.com activity logs** — On non-meeting days, he starts 70-90 min late 75% of the time. Saturday 28 Feb: arrived 90 min late, left 72 min early, completed 2 of 8 items, paused 7.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/mykhailo/mykhailo_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:62:Team meetings happen almost daily (logged on Ferrari's board). These are legitimate and explain most of the late first Monday.com actions.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/mykhailo/mykhailo_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:68:| Mon 9 Feb | Yes | 25m | 12:44 | — | **Explained** (iPhone screen refurb task, not logged in Monday) |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/mykhailo/mykhailo_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:100:| Date | Source | Message | Cross-ref (Monday.com) |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/mykhailo/mykhailo_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:232:Monday.com's time tracking is cumulative per item (not per day), so we can't measure exactly how many of those 7 hours were productive on any given day. What we can measure:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/mykhailo/mykhailo_deep_dive_2026-03-02.md:263:**CORRECTION (2 March 2026):** The original audit only checked Monday.com activity log events (column value changes) and found \"1 written update in 3 weeks.\" This was wrong. A subsequent query of item update replies (comments within items) found **83 replies by Mykhailo** in the 3-week period — an average of 4.9 per working day. Attribution works as follows:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:28:Signature matching (\"Kind regards, Michael\" / \"— Michael Ferrari\") was evaluated but deemed unnecessary: the n8n exclusion filter is sufficient to isolate human replies per the Feb 2026 audit. Cross-referencing with Monday active hours adds noise without improving accuracy and was not implemented.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:30:**Known limitation:** If another human agent ever uses the shared Support account, their replies will be misattributed to Ferrari. This has not been observed historically.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:37:| `intercom_avg_response_time_hrs` | Mean time (hours) from customer's first message in each conversation to Ferrari's first human reply. Only conversations with a measurable first-reply time are included. |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:73:| `leads_replied_personally` | Count where Ferrari (Support account, human, non-automated) sent at least one reply |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:74:| `leads_converted_to_quote` | N/A — requires Intercom-to-Monday join key (see below) |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:75:| `leads_converted_to_job` | N/A — requires Intercom-to-Monday join key (see below) |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:77:**Known limitation (conversion tracking):** There is no reliable join key linking an Intercom conversation to a Monday job. The Feb 2026 audit found Ferrari achieves ~100% conversion when handling leads personally (4/4) vs ~9% when Fin AI handles alone. To wire this up, either:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:78:- Add a Monday item ID to Intercom conversation notes at the point of booking, or\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:89:| `cs_utilisation_pct` | `gross_hours / CS_WORKING_HOURS * 100` where `CS_WORKING_HOURS = 8` | Gross hours from Monday activity log (first event to last event on the board). Values >100% indicate days longer than 8h. Does not account for actual CS task time (Intercom + calls) because per-message time data is unavailable. |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:90:| `revenue_attributed` | N/A | Would require a traceable ID linking a lead/Intercom conversation to a paid job on the Monday board. Not currently available. |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:98:| `start_time` | First Monday board activity — valid proxy for start of shift |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:99:| `finish_time` | Last Monday board activity — valid proxy for end of shift |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:101:| `items_touched` | Monday board interactions; reframed as \"Monday board items touched\" rather than repairs |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:102:| `meeting_mins` | Meeting time parsed from Ferrari's board |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:119:**Note on response times:** Average response times of 52-88 hours reflect conversations opened days or weeks earlier that Ferrari replies to on the given day — not same-day response latency. Median is a better day-level indicator.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:126:# Ferrari (CS mode — produces _with_cs_metrics.csv)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/cs_metrics_methodology.md:127:python3 scripts/team_daily_csv.py --person ferrari --from 2026-04-07 --to 2026-04-13\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/skills/performance-review/SKILL.md:17:2. **Monday board 349212843**: aggregate activity data\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/skills/performance-review/SKILL.md:43:- Any friction signals? Start with: \"of the things on your plate, which one feels stuck?\" Force prioritisation. Don't assume he's tracking previous action items; check explicitly.\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/skills/1-on-1-prep/SKILL.md:54:- Frustration they haven't voiced in Slack/Monday\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/reports/marketing-repair-profitability-brief.md:4:**Source:** Team agent analysis of Monday.com + Shopify + GSC data\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:1:# Michael Ferrari — Performance Audit\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:6:**Data Sources:** Monday.com (Board 18393875720), Intercom (admin@icorrect.co.uk / Support account)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:7:**Monday Board:** \"Ferrari's Daily Work\" — 462 items total, daily groups with time tracking\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:13:Ferrari logs 6.8–8.6 hours/day on his Monday board. The single largest bucket is \"Clients\" at 3–4.2 hours daily. **Intercom data does not support this volume.** CS Jarvis's direct API audit found ~31 manual customer-facing emails and ~12 internal notes across the week — estimated at **~75 minutes of actual composition time.** Many messages attributed to the \"Support\" account are n8n automation (device received, repair complete, courier booked), not Ferrari's manual work. Meanwhile, \"Clients\" accounts for ~18 hours across the week.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:17:Revenue-generating work (quoting, booking, invoicing) accounts for just **9% of Ferrari's 765 Monday board actions**. The remaining 91% is board admin, BM logistics, workshop intake, and data entry. He's wearing 24 different hats and the one that makes money gets the least attention. The gap between logged time and verifiable output is the core finding of this audit.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:21:## 2. MONDAY.COM — TIME TRACKING ANALYSIS\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:23:### Weekly Summary (Board: Ferrari's Daily Work)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:68:| 9702337 | Support | Admin | Shared admin account — Ferrari sends from this |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:70:Ferrari sends emails as \"Support\" (admin@icorrect.co.uk), not from a personal admin account. This means there is no way to distinguish his activity from any other admin using the same account.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:84:Two possible untracked conversions — Jordan Clark (\"I have booked online #U2TJF8MTC\") and Nizam Kabir (\"Thanks, I've now booked\") — both say they booked but neither got a `create-repair` tag. The Shopify webhook isn't reliably tagging all conversions, meaning some bookings may be slipping through without Monday items being created.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:88:**CRITICAL DISTINCTION:** Many messages sent from the \"Support\" account are **n8n automation**, not Ferrari typing. Only messages without `app_package_code: \"n8n-automations-nkor\"` are manual.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:90:| What n8n automates | What Ferrari types manually |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:103:| Internal notes (Monday item links, etc.) | ~12 | ~12 min |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:161:Warranty appointment confirmation for 26 Feb 9:30am. Tagged `create-repair` → n8n created Monday item. The one conversation this week showing proper end-to-end workflow (call → email → tag → Monday item).\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:163:**Note:** The emails Ferrari does write are well-crafted — detailed diagnostic findings, proper pricing, YouTube links, courier options. Quality is not the issue. Volume and speed are.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:213:| Metric | Logged (Monday) | Verified (Intercom) |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:228:- **Ferrari on annual leave** at the end of the previous week (pre-Feb 18). Enquiries from Feb 18-19 had neither Fin nor Ferrari responding.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:229:- **Feb 23-25 Ferrari was back and logging 7-8h/day** on Monday — but 18 contact forms still went unanswered.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:230:- **The lead bleed predates the Fin disable.** This is a Ferrari responsiveness issue, not a Fin outage issue.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:234:1. **Phone calls not captured.** Ferrari handles inbound calls. These don't show in Intercom unless he creates a follow-up email. If so, only 1 call (Vimal) resulted in a documented follow-up this week.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:236:2. **Time tracker left running.** The Monday.com time tracking may be started and not stopped, inflating hours.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:240:4. **Batch working pattern.** CS Jarvis audit shows Ferrari works in bursts — Mon 23rd had 14 messages, Tue/Wed 11-13 each, but Thu-Fri near zero. Leads from early in the week sit for days.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:246:## 5. MONDAY.COM MAIN BOARD ACTIVITY (Board 349212843)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:248:**Ferrari user ID:** 55780786\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:255:| Automation (Monday internal, user -4) | 2,678 |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:257:| **Ferrari** | **765** |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:264:### Ferrari Daily Breakdown\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:276:**Pattern matches Intercom:** Friday near-zero, Monday catch-up burst.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:278:### n8n Automation Running Under Ferrari's Profile\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:280:**16 of Ferrari's 46 item creations are n8n automation**, not manual work. The Intercom `create-repair` → Monday workflow uses Ferrari's API token, so items it creates are logged under his user ID.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:303:Each n8n creation also triggers ~26 rapid-fire column updates (Device, Client, Repair Type, etc.) within seconds — all logged under Ferrari. **~42 of Ferrari's 765 actions (5%) are pure automation.**\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:307:### Ferrari's Actual Manual Work (main board)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:309:After removing n8n automation, Ferrari's manual board work breaks down as:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:330:### Status Transitions Ferrari Makes\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:346:**Key insight:** The single biggest status action is \"Return Booked\" (39) — Back Market returns. Combined with BM-related actions (210 total across all event types), **Back Market logistics accounts for ~27% of Ferrari's main board activity.** This is admin/logistics work, not client conversion work.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:350:Ferrari's main board activity is **legitimate and substantial** — 150 actions/day, creating items, routing repairs, managing couriers, assigning technicians. This is real work. However:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:359:## 6. FERRARI MOVEMENT PATTERNS — WHAT PULLS HIM INTO CHAOS\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:363:Analysis of minute-by-minute Monday main board actions mapped by item type (Client, BM, n8n, Corporate, Parts):\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:390:Monday 23 Feb is the worst: **132 context switches across 53 different items** — switching tasks every 3-4 minutes. Wed 25 Feb had the most focused bursts (19) but still 68 switches.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:394:### The 11:41 Chaos Window (Monday 23 Feb)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:429:**77 unique BM items touched across the audit week.** Each goes through a multi-step lifecycle (receive → diagnose → repair → track → ship) and Ferrari is managing all of it.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:431:### BM Real Time vs Spread Time (Monday 23 Feb)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:442:**Total actual BM work: ~15 minutes.** Buying labels, pasting tracking numbers into Monday, clicking batch status changes. That's the entire BM workload for the day.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:444:But it's **smeared across 08:39 to 18:04 — the entire 9.5-hour working day.** Between every BM micro-task, Ferrari bounces to client items, n8n items, corporate items, then back to BM. The result is 132 context switches and zero sustained focus on anything.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:457:The CS Jarvis finding of **45h average response time** maps directly to this pattern: morning enquiries don't get touched until afternoon, and Thu/Fri arrivals sit until Monday.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:461:Ferrari isn't idle — he's **doing the wrong work at the wrong time.** The morning hours (when enquiries arrive and response speed matters most for conversion) are consumed by BM return logistics and courier dispatch. By the time he gets to Intercom, the leads are cold.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:470:## 7. FERRARI'S COMPLETE TASK BREAKDOWN\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:472:Ferrari touched **130 unique items** across the audit week, performing **765 actions**. Below is every type of work he does, broken down by item type, depth of engagement, and job function.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:476:| Item Type | Items | Actions | What Ferrari Does |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:481:| n8n (Intercom→Monday) | 19 | 140 | Completing the item setup that n8n starts |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:518:19 items arrived from Intercom via the n8n `create-repair` workflow. Each arrives with just a name and order number. Ferrari then manually fills in: Device, Requested Repairs, Repair Type, Client type, Custom Products, Status, and sometimes Tracking, Passcode, and Info Capture.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:531:Most BM items are 2–5 clicks. The batch at 11:43 on Monday — 10 items going to \"Return Booked\" simultaneously — took literally 1 second.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:533:### Monday Updates & Team Communication\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:535:Ferrari posted **37 updates** on the main board in the audit week:\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:537:| Update Type | Count | Written by Ferrari? |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:546:**32 of 37 updates are automation. Ferrari manually wrote 5 updates.**\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:569:**The revenue-generating work — quoting, booking, invoicing — is 9% of Ferrari's week.** 67 actions out of 765.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:571:### Ferrari's Daily Task List (Every Hat He Wears)\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:574:2. **Paste tracking numbers** into Monday from Royal Mail\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:576:4. **Complete n8n items** — fill in 8-12 fields on each Intercom→Monday item\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:593:21. **BM customer comms & counteroffers** — negotiating with Back Market customers on pricing/returns *(added by Ferrari)*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:594:22. **IC checks** — iCloud status checks on incoming BM devices *(added by Ferrari)*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:595:23. **SKU checks** — verifying Back Market SKUs/product listings *(added by Ferrari)*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:596:24. **Emails** — reading, triaging, and responding to Intercom inbox *(added by Ferrari)*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:598:*Tasks 21–24 added by Ferrari on review. Note: task 24 (\"Emails\") is the activity audited in Section 3 — ~31 manual emails / ~75 min composition time in the audit week (CS Jarvis verified). The gap between Ferrari's perception of email workload and the verified output is a key finding of this audit.*\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:606:**Ferrari extension:** 205\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:615:| Ferrari (Ext 205) answered | 1,134 (61% of all answered) |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:616:| Average calls/week (Ferrari) | 45 |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:620:### Ferrari Call Volume — Weekly Trend\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:679:- Ferrari was responsible for the rollout but was not maintaining or improving it\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:693:3. **Require Intercom conversation links.** Every client task on Monday must link to the corresponding Intercom conversation or have a note explaining what happened.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:697:4. **Implement conversion tracking.** Track: Enquiry → Quote Sent → Booking Made → Device Received → Repair Complete → Payment. This is the pipeline Ferrari should be managing.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:701:6. **Separate admin accounts.** Ferrari should have his own Intercom admin account, not share \"Support\". This makes individual activity trackable.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:705:7. **Define measurable KPIs for Ferrari's role:**\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:712:8. **Regular audit cadence.** Weekly cross-reference of Monday time tracking vs Intercom output, automated via agent.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:714:9. **Migrate n8n/agent automations to a service account.** Currently n8n workflows use individual API tokens (Ferrari's for Intercom→Monday, Ricky's for BM→Monday). All automation should run under a dedicated service account (e.g., \"Systems Manager\" or new \"Automation\" user) so human activity audits aren't polluted by bot actions.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:716:10. **Separate Ferrari's personal board categories.** The current \"Clients\" bucket should be broken into: Client Calls, Client Emails, Quotes, BM Admin, Courier Management, Board Admin. This matches what the main board activity data shows he's actually doing.\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:724:| Source | What It Shows | Ferrari's Verified Output |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:726:| **Monday personal board** (self-reported) | 18h/week on \"Clients\" | Unverifiable — single opaque bucket |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:729:| **Monday main board** (activity logs) | 765 actions, 150 items touched | Legitimate admin work — but not \"Clients\" |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:730:| **Monday movement patterns** (minute-by-minute) | 132 context switches on peak day, BM dominates mornings | Enquiries ignored 08:00–14:00 while doing BM logistics |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:738:| Monday board admin (status changes, etc.) | 2-3h |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:740:| **Monday logged total** | **~38h/week** |\n/home/ricky/builds/team-audits/reports/ferrari/ferrari_audit_2026-02-26.md:748:- Shared \"Support\" Intercom account (admin 9702337) — some messages attributed to Ferrari may be from other admins.
