His update replies show him routing work to Mykhailo, Andreas, Roni, and Ferrari, making BER decisions, flagging MDM-locked devices before purchase, and escalating technical edge cases to Ricky.\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/docs/team-members/safan-patel/profile.md:34:- Prefers to communicate via Monday updates rather than Slack\n/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/team/workspace/docs/team-members/safan-patel/profile.md:78:*Sources: Deep dive audit (2026-03-02), Slack DM history, Monday.com activity logs*\n",
/home/ricky/.openclaw/agents/main/workspace/state/sessions/agent%3Acodex%3Aacp%3Aa4a14a7b-c134-49c3-986f-fb2d898890d2%3Aoneshot%3A750490d1-07ae-44ed-a6b5-f56a5a1bd579.json:1232:              "stdout": "# Michael Ferrari — Team Profile\n*Last updated: 2026-03-04*\n\n## Role\nClient Services (Remote, Italy) + BM Operations + Website/Systems Dev\n\n## The Short Version\nFerrari is talented, proactive, and drowning. He has 24 different hats, every payment needs Ricky's approval, and his mornings are consumed by BM admin exactly when customer enquiries arrive. The result: 45.3h average response time, 30% of leads get a reply, ~£150k/year in missed revenue. This isn't a motivation problem — it's a structural one. The fix is role definition, automation, and giving him spending authority up to a threshold.\n\n---\n\n## Performance (Feb 18-25, 2026 — audit week)\n- **Intercom response rate:** 30% of 80 contact form leads got a human reply\n- **No-reply rate:** 14% got no reply at all; 56% got Fin AI only (0 confirmed conversions)\n- **Average response time:** 45.3h (median 22.4h)\n- **Invoices sent:** 4 in 5 working days\n- **Monday board actions:** 765 in a week — but 59% are board admin, 16% BM logistics. Only 9% is revenue-generating client work\n- **Phone:** Answers 61% of all calls (45/week avg), handles mornings well but has lunch gap (26% answer rate at noon)\n- **Zendesk baseline comparison:** Was at 284 messages/week peak, now ~31 manual Intercom emails. 89% decline in output volume\n\n## The Core Problem: BM Eats His Mornings\n| Time Block | What He's Doing | What's Happening |\n|-----------|-----------------|-----------------|\n| 08:30–12:00 | BM returns, tracking numbers, logistics | Customer enquiries arriving, no one responding |\n| 14:00–17:00 | Client work picks up | Leads are 4-6 hours cold |\n\nActual BM admin time: ~15 minutes per day (batch operations, seconds per action). But it's smeared across the entire morning in 54-second windows between context switches. 132 context switches on one Monday. He's not wasting time — he's disorganised, and it's costing leads.\n\n## The Approval Bottleneck\nHe cannot approve a payment, order parts, send a label, or make most client decisions without Ricky's approval. Ricky is in Bali, 8 hours ahead. A decision Ferrari needs at 10am UK is 6pm Bali. This is structural and it's slowing everything.\n\n---\n\n## Communication Style\n- Operational and detailed in DMs — flags issues, spots errors (BM wrong model listings, listings at a loss, BM validation gaps)\n- Proactively identifies problems before Ricky sees them\n- Excited about Jarvis automation — genuinely invested in the system\n- Will call BM company directly and explore B2B relationships (proactive)\n- Communicates quality emails when he writes them — the issue is volume and speed, not quality\n- Gets distracted and loses track of parallel workstreams — needs clear task management\n\n## What He Needs\n1. **Spending authority up to £500** — stops the approval loop for everyday purchases (parts, labels, boxes)\n2. **BM admin properly batched/automated** — labels at 08:30, tracking at 11:00, shipping at 17:30. Three blocks, 15 min total, morning free for Intercom\n3. **Protected 09:00-12:00 block** — Intercom and calls only. No BM, no board admin\n4. **Fewer hats** — key ones to remove: walk-in intake (→ Adil), parts ordering (→ Roni), BM counteroffers (→ Roni), basic board admin (→ automation)\n5. **Separate Intercom account** — can't track his individual performance currently (uses shared \"Support\" account)\n6. **Clear KPIs** — he tracks time but not outcomes. He needs 5 measurable targets (see below)\n\n## Proposed KPIs\n| KPI | Target | Current |\n|-----|--------|---------|\n| Enquiry response time | <2h (09:00-17:00) | 45.3h avg |\n| Contact form → quote rate | >60% | ~21% |\n| Quote → booking rate | >40% | ~24% |\n| Invoices/week | 10+ | 4 |\n| Intercom reply rate | >90% of leads | 30% |\n\n## Personal Context\n- Based in Italy — remote worker\n- Gets distracted easily, hyper-focuses on one project and loses track of others\n- Genuinely excited about Jarvis and what it can do — this is a positive leverage point\n- Fin AI rollout was his responsibility — it went unsupervised, had 0 confirmed conversions, frustrated customers. He needs to own the fix.\n- Was on leave prior to Feb 18 — first week back is always a catch-up\n- Proactively spots issues that others miss (BM listing errors, model mismatches, pricing at a loss)\n\n## What He's Doing Well\n- Highest Monday board action count among humans (765/week — Ferrari is the operational engine of the board)\n- 61% of all answered calls — he handles phone well\n- Catches systematic errors (BM model mismatches, Jarvis listing wrong specs)\n- Writes high-quality diagnostic emails when he does them (the issue is speed, not quality)\n- Proactively flags process gaps (BM validation not auto-triggering, counteroffer flow broken)\n- Invested in building better systems — not just managing chaos\n\n## Watch Points\n- **£150k/year revenue at risk** from lead response failures — this is the most urgent issue in the company\n- **Fin AI left unsupervised** — 45 leads handled by bot with 0 conversions and frustrated customers\n- **Time tracking black box** — \"Clients\" logged as 3-4.2h/day but only ~1.3h verifiable in Intercom\n- **Context switching** — 132 switches in one day, switching every 3-4 minutes. Needs structural blocks, not willpower\n- **Parallel workstream tracking** — hyper-focuses on one thing (current: Jarvis/systems) and lets others slip (Intercom responses)\n\n## Conversation Guide\n**Frame it as a redesign, not a performance review.** The data isn't \"you're failing\" — it's \"the role is broken and we need to fix it together.\"\n\n**Lead with the revenue number:** \"I've calculated that we're losing around £150k a year in missed leads. That's not because you're doing a bad job — it's because BM is eating your mornings and you need approval for everything. Let's fix the structure.\"\n\n**The three questions to answer together:**\n1. What hats can we take off you right now?\n2. Where is your time best spent — and what blocks that?\n3. What spending authority do you need to move faster?\n\n**Then set the KPIs** — give him ownership of the 5 metrics above with a 4-week review.\n\n---\n\n*Sources: Ferrari complete audit (2026-02-26), Slack DM history, Intercom API (CS Jarvis), TeleSphere CDR, Zendesk historical*\n",

What hats can we take off you right now?\n2. Where is your time best spent — and what blocks that?\n3. What spending authority do you need to move faster?\n\n**Then set the KPIs** — give him ownership of the 5 metrics above with a 4-week review.\n\n---\n\n*Sources: Ferrari complete audit (2026-02-26), Slack DM history, Intercom API (CS Jarvis), TeleSphere CDR, Zendesk historical*\n",

/home/ricky/.openclaw/shared/VISION.md:17:Ricky knows this industry and its systems better than anyone playing at this level.
